title: Digital Services Taxes in Europe, 2026
summary: Currently, about half of all European OECD countries have either announced, proposed, or implemented a digital services tax. Because these taxes mainly impact US companies and are thus perceived as discriminatory, the US responded with retaliatory tariff threats.
ai_summary: Currently, about half of all European OECD countries have either announced, proposed, or implemented a digital services tax. Because these taxes mainly impact US companies and are thus perceived as discriminatory, the US responded with retaliatory tariff threats.

title: ITAT Chennai Allowed Section 80-IA Deduction as Consumer Tariff Held Correct Benchmark
summary: The Tribunal found that the transfer pricing adjustment was incorrectly computed using SEB sale rates. It allowed deduction based on consumer tariff rates. The decision clarifies benchmarking for captive consumption. The post ITAT Chennai Allowed Section 80-IA Deduction as Consumer Tariff Held Correct Benchmark appeared first on TaxGuru .

title: Delay in Filing Form 10 Cannot Alone Defeat Section 11(2) Exemption Claim: ITAT Visakhapatnam
summary: The Tribunal ruled that exemption under Section 11(2) should not be denied solely due to delayed filing of Form-10. It directed reconsideration where the form was available during assessment. The post Delay in Filing Form 10 Cannot Alone Defeat Section 11(2) Exemption Claim: ITAT Visakhapatnam appeared first on TaxGuru .

title: Channel owners cannot be taken as comparables for distributors for transfer pricing purpose
summary: ITAT Delhi held that companies owning and operating channels cannot be compared with distribution company for the purpose of transfer pricing. Accordingly, directs TPO to exclude such comparables for benchmarking distribution segment and determination of ALP. The post Channel owners cannot be taken as comparables for distributors for transfer pricing purpose appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: ITAT Delhi held that companies owning and operating channels cannot be compared with distribution company for the purpose of transfer pricing. title: Pass-Through Costs Excluded From Transfer Pricing PLI as No Value Addition Was Performed: ITAT Mumbai
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that payments made to third-party vendors on a cost-to-cost basis, without any value addition by the assessee, should be treated as pass-through costs and excluded from PLI computation. The post Pass-Through Costs Excluded From Transfer Pricing PLI as No Value Addition Was Performed: ITAT Mumbai appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: ITAT Mumbai held that payments made to third-party vendors on a cost-to-cost basis, without any value addition by the assessee, should be treated as pass-through costs and excluded from PLI computation. title: Cloud Service Payments Not Taxable as Royalty Due to No Transfer of IP Rights: SC
summary: The Supreme Court affirmed that payments for cloud computing services are not royalty where no intellectual property rights are transferred. It held that mere access to services does not amount to use of equipment or technology under tax law. The post Cloud Service Payments Not Taxable as Royalty Due to No Transfer of IP Rights: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

title: Only Legitimate Tax Can Be Collected; ITAT Restores BBC Distribution Fee Royalty Taxability Issue
summary: The Tribunal set aside the dismissal of a delayed appeal, holding that the issue of distribution fee taxability requires fresh examination on merits. The case was remanded to the Assessing Officer for reconsideration with proper hearing. The post Only Legitimate Tax Can Be Collected; ITAT Restores BBC Distribution Fee Royalty Taxability Issue appeared first on TaxGuru .
